title: API Breaking-Change Report
description: Produced by api-breaking-change-analyzer. Consumes two api-contract.json snapshots — `before` and `after` — and emits the detected differences classified by breaking-ness and severity. Phase 3 ships the contract only — first-class breaking-change rules cover OpenAPI; tRPC and GraphQL get presence + heuristic-count diffs with full breaking-change analysis deferred to Phase 6+ stack reviewers. Plan, not verdict; downstream Design / API-review agents consume it to inform decisions.

inputs.description: One entry per api-contract.json snapshot the analyzer attempted to consume — `before` (the baseline) and `after` (the candidate change set). Both expected for a real diff; either may be absent (with status reflecting that).

inputs_consumed.description: Count of inputs[] entries with available=true. <= len(inputs).

analysis_status.description: analyzed = both snapshots loaded and a diff was produced; no_after = before loaded but no candidate snapshot supplied (baseline view only); no_before = no baseline to compare against (all after-contracts surface as `contract_added`, none `breaking`); skipped = preconditions not met (e.g. both inputs unloadable).

role.description: Which side of the diff this snapshot represents. At most one entry per role; the analyzer expects exactly one of each for an `analyzed` outcome.

generated_at.description: The api-contract.json snapshot's own generated_at. Required when available=true; omitted when available=false.

note.description: Per-input note (e.g. 'snapshot missing', 'snapshot did not parse'). Omitted when available=true and the snapshot loaded cleanly.

kind.description: Open-vocabulary category. Common Phase 3 values — contract_added, contract_removed, contract_kind_mismatch, path_added, path_removed, method_added, method_removed, operationId_changed, info_version_changed, router_added, router_removed, types_count_changed, queries_count_changed, mutations_count_changed, subscriptions_count_changed, snapshot_invalid.

object.description: Stable identifier of the contract object the change touches — e.g. `api/openapi.yaml` (contract), `GET /users/{id}` (path+method), `listUsers` (operationId), `src/server/routers/_app.ts:userRouter` (tRPC router), `schema/schema.graphql` (GraphQL contract).

breaking.description: breaking = a conformant client written against `before` will fail against `after` (path/method removed, contract removed). potentially-breaking = client may fail depending on usage (operationId renamed, tRPC router renamed, GraphQL counts decreased). non-breaking = additive only (path/method added, info_version bumped, summary text changed, GraphQL counts increased).

severity.description: high = a breaking change to a contract with significant traffic/exposure (Phase 6+ reviewers will refine using usage data); medium = potentially-breaking, or breaking on low-traffic paths; low = non-breaking changes that operators may still want to know about.

evidence.description: Pointers to specific snapshot fields supporting this change — contract source_file paths, OpenAPI path+method tuples, router names, GraphQL count tuples. A change without evidence is opinion, not a diff.
